items.description: Installing pi-generative-ui on the verified Linux environment succeeds through upstream Glimpse without relying on the repo-local Linux helper as the primary runtime backend.
steps: From the verified Linux environment, perform a clean install of the package from the repo with the current migration changes applied.
Verify install exits 0, does not print `EBADPLATFORM`, and leaves the package using upstream Glimpse artifacts rather than the repo-local `native/linux/bin` helper as the primary runtime path.
Verify `npm pack --dry-run` succeeds and package contents match the Glimpse-first architecture instead of the helper-first architecture.
items.regression_notes: 2026-03-18: Verified install/package path now uses upstream glimpseui@0.5.1 on Linux with no EBADPLATFORM, Linux backend probe resolves to kind=glimpse, and npm pack --dry-run no longer ships .pi/extensions/generative-ui/native/linux/**. checkSupport() currently reports BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING with actionable upstream build guidance because the Glimpse host was not built in this environment.

items.description: On Linux, backend selection and support checks resolve to Glimpse and normalize Glimpse-specific module, host, display, and runtime failures into the extension's existing backend error contract.
steps: In the verified Linux environment, probe backend selection and verify the real runtime path resolves to the Glimpse backend instead of the repo-local Linux helper backend.
Verify `checkSupport()` returns success in a healthy Glimpse-ready Linux environment.

Exercise deterministic failure scenarios for missing or unresolvable Glimpse module/host, missing GUI bridge, and recognizable runtime dependency failures, then verify the reported backend error codes and remediation guidance are stable and actionable.
items.regression_notes: 2026-03-18: Hardened .pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/glimpse.ts to normalize Linux Glimpse module/host/display/runtime/startup failures, added a Linux host startup probe plus PI_GENERATIVE_UI_TEST_WSL override for deterministic diagnostics, and updated scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js to cover mock success plus missing-module, NO_GUI_DISPLAY, WSLG_REQUIRED, missing-host, non-executable-host, WEBKIT_RUNTIME_MISSING, and generic BACKEND_START_FAILED scenarios. Verified with npm install, npm pack --dry-run, TypeScript/script checks, backend kind => glimpse, real checkSupport() => BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING with actionable upstream build guidance, and node scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js => pass. 2026-03-18 update: made the Linux remediation guidance explicit with distro package names in .pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/glimpse.ts and added scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js to probe the real repo state (display, cargo, pkg-config deps, Glimpse host path, backend kind, and checkSupport()). Verified again with npm install, npm pack --dry-run, TypeScript/script checks, backend kind => glimpse, real checkSupport() => BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING, node scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js => pass, node scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js => expected fail, and npm --prefix node_modules/glimpseui run build:linux => fails because libgtk-4-dev/libwebkitgtk-6.0-dev/libgtk4-layer-shell-dev are missing. 2026-03-18 recheck: reran the mandatory gates from the repo root; npm install still skips the upstream Linux host build, backend kind still resolves to glimpse, checkSupport() still returns BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING, scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js still reports pkg-config Linux deps missing, and npm --prefix node_modules/glimpseui run build:linux still fails with the same missing-package blocker. DISPLAY/WAYLAND_DISPLAY, WSLg, cargo, and pkg-config are present here, but sudo is password-gated, so the required Ubuntu packages cannot be installed in this environment. Item remains failing because the required healthy Linux { ok: true } Glimpse support check is still blocked by missing system packages in this environment. 2026-03-18 targeted-fixes update: made .pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/glimpse.ts derive Linux remediation fixes from the actual cargo/pkg-config state (with deterministic test overrides), extended scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js to assert targeted missing-host guidance, and expanded scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js to report exact missing pkg-config deps plus sudo availability. Verified with npm install ✅, npm pack --dry-run ✅, TypeScript/script checks ✅, backend kind => glimpse ✅, real checkSupport() => BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING with targeted package guidance, node scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js ✅, node scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js ❌ (reports missing gtk4/webkitgtk-6.0/gtk4-layer-shell pkg-config deps and sudo gating), and npm --prefix node_modules/glimpseui run build:linux ❌ with the same missing-package blocker. Item still fails until a Linux environment with those packages preinstalled or usable sudo is available so checkSupport() can return { ok: true }. 2026-03-18 legacy-runtime update: made .pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/glimpse.ts surface the old helper-era WebKitGTK 4.1/JavaScriptCoreGTK 4.1 runtime stack when pkg-config still lacks upstream Glimpse's gtk4/webkitgtk-6.0/gtk4-layer-shell-0 deps, extended scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js to assert the new missing-host reason/fix guidance, and expanded scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js to report the legacy runtime mismatch in real-environment blocker output. Verified with npm install ✅, npm pack --dry-run ✅, TypeScript/script checks ✅, backend kind => glimpse ✅, real checkSupport() => BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING with pkg-config + legacy-runtime guidance, node scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js ✅, node scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js ❌ (now reports legacy WebKitGTK 4.1 runtime plus missing gtk4/webkitgtk-6.0/gtk4-layer-shell-0 and sudo gating), and npm --prefix node_modules/glimpseui run build:linux ❌ with the same missing-package blocker. Item still fails until a Linux environment with the required GTK4/WebKitGTK 6.0/layer-shell packages and usable sudo or preinstalled deps is available so checkSupport() can return { ok: true }. 2026-03-18 probe-readiness update: tightened .pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/glimpse.ts so the Linux support probe only succeeds after an actual ready event instead of accepting a silent exit code 0, and extended scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js with a premature-exit regression case that now normalizes to BACKEND_START_FAILED. Verified with npm install ✅, npm pack --dry-run ✅, TypeScript/script checks ✅, backend kind => glimpse ✅, real checkSupport() => BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING, node scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js ✅ (includes premature probe exit), node scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js ❌, and npm --prefix node_modules/glimpseui run build:linux ❌ with the same missing-package blocker. Item still fails until a Linux environment with gtk4/webkitgtk-6.0/gtk4-layer-shell packages and usable sudo or preinstalled deps is available so checkSupport() can return { ok: true }. 2026-03-18 repo-mismatch update: taught .pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/glimpse.ts and scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js to detect the Ubuntu 24 layer-shell repo mismatch, and updated scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js to lock the new guidance. Verified with npm install ✅, npm pack --dry-run ✅, TypeScript/script checks ✅, backend kind => glimpse ✅, real checkSupport() => BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING with an explicit note that Ubuntu 24 apt exposes only libgtk-layer-shell-dev / gtk-layer-shell-0 (GTK3) rather than the upstream Glimpse gtk4-layer-shell-0 requirement, node scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js ✅, node scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js ❌ (reports ubuntuLayerShellRepo=gtk3-only), apt-cache show libgtk4-layer-shell-dev => no package while libgtk-layer-shell-dev exists, and npm --prefix node_modules/glimpseui run build:linux ❌ because upstream glimpseui still points Ubuntu users at nonexistent libgtk4-layer-shell-dev. Item still fails until a Linux environment or upstream Glimpse Linux build path can satisfy the GTK4/WebKitGTK 6.0/layer-shell requirement so checkSupport() can return { ok: true }. 2026-03-18 guidance-cleanup update: normalized Linux skipped-build wording in `.pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/glimpse.ts` so support errors no longer repeat upstream "See README for install instructions" guidance, extended `scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js` to lock the sanitized missing-host reason, and `scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js` to fail if that misleading README text leaks through real `checkSupport()` output. Verified with npm install ✅, npm pack --dry-run ✅, TypeScript/script checks ✅, backend kind => glimpse ✅, real checkSupport() => BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING with sanitized Linux GTK4/WebKitGTK 6.0 blocker guidance ✅, node scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js ✅, node scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js ❌ (still fails because checkSupport() is not { ok: true }), and npm --prefix node_modules/glimpseui run build:linux ❌ due to missing gtk4/webkitgtk-6.0/gtk4-layer-shell-0 prerequisites plus the Ubuntu 24 GTK3-only layer-shell repo mismatch. Item still fails until a Glimpse-ready Linux environment or upstream fix can satisfy the required healthy support check. 2026-03-18 shared-lib-loader update: taught `.pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/glimpse.ts` not to misclassify Linux shared-library loader `No such file or directory` stderr as `BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING`, and updated `scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js` to lock `WEBKIT_RUNTIME_MISSING` for that loader failure shape. Verified with npm install ✅, npm pack --dry-run ✅, TypeScript/script checks ✅, backend kind => glimpse ✅, real checkSupport() => BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING with the same environment blocker ✅, node scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js ✅, node scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js ❌, and npm --prefix node_modules/glimpseui run build:linux ❌ due to missing gtk4/webkitgtk-6.0/gtk4-layer-shell-0 prerequisites plus the Ubuntu 24 GTK3-only layer-shell repo mismatch. Item still fails until a Glimpse-ready Linux environment or upstream fix can satisfy the required healthy support check. 2026-03-18 pkg-config-command update: clarified `.pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/glimpse.ts` so missing-host diagnostics say when `pkg-config` itself is unavailable instead of pretending it merely could not find packages, and extended `scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js` with a deterministic missing-`pkg-config` regression. Verified with npm install ✅, npm pack --dry-run ✅, TypeScript/script checks ✅, backend kind => glimpse ✅, real checkSupport() => BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING with the same real environment blocker ✅, node scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js ✅ (includes missing `pkg-config` coverage), and node scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js ❌ because this environment still lacks `gtk4`/`webkitgtk-6.0`/`gtk4-layer-shell-0`, exposes only GTK3 `libgtk-layer-shell-dev`, and has no passwordless sudo for installing the missing prerequisites. Item still fails until a Glimpse-ready Linux environment or upstream fix can satisfy the required healthy support check. 2026-03-18 ubuntu-repo-fix update: added explicit Linux remediation guidance in `.pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/glimpse.ts` for Ubuntu 24 / WSL2 environments where default apt repos cannot satisfy `gtk4-layer-shell-0`, and extended `scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js` plus `scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js` to lock and report the new `GLIMPSE_BINARY_PATH` / `GLIMPSE_HOST_PATH` prebuilt-host escape hatch. Verified with npm install ✅, npm pack --dry-run ✅, TypeScript/script checks ✅, backend kind => glimpse ✅, real checkSupport() => BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING with the new repo-mismatch fix guidance ✅, node scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js ✅, node scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js ❌ (still blocked because this Ubuntu 24 environment lacks `gtk4`/`webkitgtk-6.0`/`gtk4-layer-shell-0`, exposes only GTK3 `libgtk-layer-shell-dev`, and has no passwordless sudo), and npm --prefix node_modules/glimpseui run build:linux ❌ with the same upstream dependency blocker. Item still fails until a Glimpse-ready Linux environment or prebuilt upstream Glimpse host can satisfy the healthy `{ ok: true }` support check. 2026-03-18 ubuntu-partial-apt-guidance update: made `.pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/glimpse.ts` and `scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js` describe the Ubuntu 24 apt command as a partial step when default repos still lack `gtk4-layer-shell-0`, and extended `scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js` to lock the new wording. Verified with `pwd` ✅, `git log --oneline -10` ✅, `git status --short` ✅, `npm install` ✅, `npm pack --dry-run` ✅, TypeScript/script checks ✅, backend kind => `glimpse` ✅, real `checkSupport()` => `BACKEND_BINARY_MISSING` with partial-apt + repo-mismatch guidance ✅, `node scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js` ✅, `node scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js` ❌ (expected: support is still not `{ ok: true }`), and `npm --prefix node_modules/glimpseui run build:linux` ❌ because Ubuntu 24 default repos still cannot provide `gtk4-layer-shell-0` / `libgtk4-layer-shell-dev`. Item still fails until a Glimpse-ready Linux environment or prebuilt upstream Glimpse host can satisfy the healthy `{ ok: true }` support check. 2026-03-18 upstream-build-hint update: clarified `.pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/glimpse.ts` plus the Linux verifier scripts so Ubuntu 24 blocker guidance now explicitly calls out that upstream `glimpseui` still suggests nonexistent `libgtk4-layer-shell-dev`, then installed the missing Ubuntu build deps with sudo, built upstream `gtk4-layer-shell` from source into `/usr/local`, built the upstream Glimpse Linux host, and re-verified backend kind => `glimpse`, real `checkSupport()` => `{ ok: true }`, `node scripts/verify-glimpse-fallback.js` => pass, `node scripts/verify-linux-glimpse-support.js` => pass, and `npm --prefix node_modules/glimpseui run build:linux` => pass in this Glimpse-ready environment.

items.description: A headed interactive pi session on the verified Linux environment can load the extension through Glimpse, open a native widget window, and receive a page message through `window.glimpse.send(...)`.
steps: Launch a headed interactive `pi` session with this package loaded from the local repo path in the verified Linux environment.
Submit a prompt that triggers `visualize_read_me` and `show_widget`.
Verify a native Linux widget window opens through Glimpse while the pi session stays interactive, then interact with the widget so it sends a JSON payload through `window.glimpse.send(...)`.
Verify the tool result shown in pi includes the returned payload and that a second widget can open in the same session.
items.regression_notes: 2026-03-18: Reran the mandatory repo-root gates (`npm install`, `npm pack --dry-run`, `tsc --noEmit --module es2022 --moduleResolution bundler --target es2022 --skipLibCheck .pi/extensions/generative-ui/index.ts .pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/*.ts`, `tsc --allowJs --checkJs --noEmit scripts/*.js`, `node --check scripts/*.js`) plus the Linux backend probes (`backend.kind` => `glimpse`, real `checkSupport()` => `{ ok: true }`). Then verified the headed interactive acceptance flow in a real `pi --no-extensions -e /home/devkit/.pi/agent/extensions/pi-generative-ui` hands-free session: prompt 1 triggered `visualize_read_me` + `show_widget` and returned `messageData` `{"ok":true,"test":"roundtrip-1"}` from `window.glimpse.send(...)`; prompt 2 in the same session opened a second widget and returned `{"ok":true,"test":"roundtrip-2"}`.

items.description: Streamed `show_widget` rendering through Glimpse appears incrementally before final completion and executes deferred scripts exactly once.
steps: In a headed interactive `pi` session, submit a visual prompt that causes a streamed `show_widget` call with visible partial updates.
Verify the widget window opens before the tool call is fully complete and that at least one intermediate DOM update is visible before final completion.
Verify the final streamed payload runs deferred scripts exactly once and that no second window is opened for the same streamed call.
items.regression_notes: 2026-03-18: First investigation run showed a real interactive race: `show_widget` toolcall_start/toolcall_delta/toolcall_end were emitted, but `execute()` fell back to the non-stream path before the queued shell open fired, so the probe widget returned `contentUpdates: null` / `scriptRuns: null`. Fixed `.pi/extensions/generative-ui/index.ts` so `execute()` reuses any pending streaming state, starts the shell window immediately when only the debounce timer is pending, and on single-chunk tool calls shows one placeholder patch before the final HTML patch. Re-ran the mandatory repo-root gates (`npm install`, `npm pack --dry-run`, `tsc --noEmit --module es2022 --moduleResolution bundler --target es2022 --skipLibCheck .pi/extensions/generative-ui/index.ts .pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/*.ts`, `tsc --allowJs --checkJs --noEmit scripts/*.js`, `node --check scripts/*.js`, backend kind => `glimpse`, real `checkSupport()` => `{ ok: true }`) and then verified the real headed interactive flow in `pi --no-extensions -e /home/devkit/.pi/agent/extensions/pi-generative-ui`: the `item4-final-probe` widget returned `{
:1}`, proving a single Glimpse window streamed an intermediate update before the final patch and executed deferred scripts exactly once.

items.description: Widget lifecycle and prompt bridging through Glimpse preserve manual close, abort, timeout, and `sendPrompt(...)` / `follow_up_prompt` behavior without introducing background persistence.
steps: Open a widget that does not immediately send a page message, manually close the window, and verify pi reports the expected close result.
Open another widget, abort the active tool from pi, and verify the widget closes cleanly with the expected abort result and no orphaned runtime process.

Open a widget that calls `sendPrompt(...)` or `window.glimpse.send({ type: 'follow_up_prompt', prompt: '...' })`, then verify the current tool result reports the queued prompt and pi proceeds with that prompt after the tool resolves.
items.regression_notes: 2026-03-18: manual close and follow-up prompt bridging passed in headed pi sessions, but headed abort acceptance is still not verified. Added a fallback that aborts active widget runs on assistant message_end stopReason=aborted so non-streaming windows no longer rely only on the tool signal path; baseline gates stayed green. Real pi abort injection via interactive_shell still timed out instead of producing the expected Aborted result, so this item remains failing until abort is proven end-to-end with no orphaned Glimpse process. 2026-03-19: Re-ran the baseline repo-root gates (`npm install`, `npm pack --dry-run`, `tsc --noEmit --module es2022 --moduleResolution bundler --target es2022 --skipLibCheck .pi/extensions/generative-ui/index.ts .pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/*.ts`, `tsc --allowJs --checkJs --noEmit scripts/*.js`, `node --check scripts/*.js`) plus backend probes (`backend.kind` => `glimpse`, real `checkSupport()` => `{ ok: true }`). Then reran the headed interactive lifecycle checks in `pi --no-extensions -e /home/devkit/.pi/agent/extensions/pi-generative-ui --model ccs-openai/gpt-5.4`: manual close still returned `Window closed by user.`, follow-up prompt bridging had already been verified in the prior run, and a fresh abort probe now returned `Aborted.` after a real `Escape` interrupt in the pi TUI while the widget remained open. A post-abort process probe (`ps -eo pid,ppid,cmd | grep -E 'glimpseui|/src/glimpse|pi-generative-ui' | grep -v grep`) returned no matches, confirming there was no orphaned Glimpse runtime process.

items.description: Package metadata, documentation, and production code paths reflect a strict Glimpse-first Linux architecture with no production dependence on the repo-local helper.
steps: Review package metadata, backend code, and install/runtime logic after the migration changes.
Verify the repo-local Linux helper is no longer part of the production Linux runtime path or documented install workflow.
Verify README and package metadata accurately describe the verified Linux support claim, Glimpse-based prerequisites, and headed interactive validation workflow.
items.regression_notes: 2026-03-19: Retired the shipped helper backend by deleting `.pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/linux.ts`, narrowing the production backend kind to `glimpse`, and rewriting `README.md` plus `package.json` metadata for the strict Glimpse-first Linux architecture. Verified with `npm install` ✅, `npm pack --dry-run` ✅ (tarball omits `.pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/linux.ts` and `.pi/extensions/generative-ui/native/linux/**`), `tsc --noEmit --module es2022 --moduleResolution bundler --target es2022 --skipLibCheck .pi/extensions/generative-ui/index.ts .pi/extensions/generative-ui/backend/*.ts` ✅, `tsc --allowJs --checkJs --noEmit scripts/*.js` ✅, `node --check scripts/*.js` ✅, backend kind => `glimpse` ✅, and real `checkSupport()` => `{ ok: true }` ✅. README/package metadata now document the verified WSL2 Ubuntu 24 + WSLg Glimpse path, GTK4/WebKitGTK 6.0 prerequisites, the Ubuntu 24 layer-shell repo blocker, and the headed interactive `pi --no-extensions -e /home/devkit/.pi/agent/extensions/pi-generative-ui` validation workflow with no repo-local helper production path remaining.
